_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Interpret cyberspace Warning Orders (WARNORDs), Alert Orders (ALERTORD), and Fragmentary Orders (FRAGORDs).
reference_document_element_description: Implement platform monitoring and alerting for availability, performance, cost, and security events, with defined escalation procedures.
comments: DCWF task intersects with WIDAI DA domain. DA-T-009 is the strongest semantic match. Secondary: RC-K-011 (RC, cosine: 0.354), SP-T-007 (SP, cosine: 0.314).
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark.
primary_method.significance: STS 4.5/10 (raw 0.449). No substantive shared vocabulary — relationship is purely semantic. DCWF-only terms [alert, alertord, cyberspace, fragmentary, fragords, interpret] vs WIDAI-only terms [alerting, availability, cost, defined, escalation, events] define the semantic distance between the descriptions.

bertscore.significance: BERTScore F1=0.833 (P=0.809, R=0.858). R exceeds P by 0.049 — the WIDAI KSA's 16 words are well-captured by the DCWF element's 12 words, but the DCWF element contains broader scope not fully reflected in the matched WIDAI KSA.

nli_cross_encoder.significance: NLI: neutral-dominant (99.7%). "Interpret cyberspace Warning Orders (WARNORDs), Alert Orders (ALERTORD), and Fra" and "Implement platform monitoring and alerting for availability, performance, cost," are related but neither implies nor contradicts the other. Standard result for cross-framework mapping where two independent frameworks describe overlapping competencies with independent vocabulary.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: Bi-encoder 0.386 < STS 0.449 (gap -0.063). Cross-attention finds deeper alignment than independent encoding. The relationship depends on cross-text reasoning — concept-level mapping between [alert, alertord, cyberspace] and [alerting, availability, cost] that bi-encoders structurally cannot capture.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens).
jaccard_token.significance: Jaccard 4.2%. Zero substantive shared tokens. The entire relationship is semantic — no lexical trace. DCWF terms: [alert, alertord, cyberspace, fragmentary, fragords, interpret]. WIDAI terms: [alerting, availability, cost, defined, escalation, events].
